text: Disable switch s1.
Enable switch s1.

Turn on switch s3.
Reboot switch s2.
Shut down port 5 on switch s4.
Set the nickname of s1 to 'core-switch-01'.
Set the power state of s2 to off.
Backup the current configuration of s5.
Restore the configuration for s5.
Update the firmware on core-switch-01.
Turn on historical logging.
Install a new flow entry on s2.
Modify flow entry 12 on switch s2.
Delete the flow entry with ID 45 on s1.
Create a new group entry on s7.
Install a rate-limiting meter on s3 using defaule setting
Reroute traffic from h1 to h5 via s4.
Show the CPU utilization for all switches.

What's the network's total power use?
How much power is switch s4 using?
What is the bandwidth usage from s1 to s2 on port eth1 for 60 seconds?
Which are the top 5 most congested links?
Which 10 IPs use the most bandwidth?
What's the latency between s2 and s3?
Show packet loss for the link between s1-s2.
Get stats for port 8 on switch s6.
Show memory usage for switch s1.
What's switch s4 temp
How long has s1 been online?
Check the health status of router s1.
Show real-time traffic s2 port 4 for 90 seconds.
Show me the network topology.
List all hosts on the network.
What is the path from host h2 to host h7?
How many switches from path h1 to h8?
List all ports on switch s5.
What are the capabilities of switch s1?
Display the ARP table for s1.
Show the MAC address table for s2.
List the top 10 flows.
How many active flows are in the network?
How many flow entries are in s5?
List all flow entries in switch s7.

Show the latest logs from firewall f1.
Clear all system logs on switch s3.
Block traffic from 192.168.1.100.
The whole network feels really sluggish this morning, something is draining the bandwidth.
Users are complaining about choppy video calls. Find out what's causing the bottleneck.
I think we have a broadcast storm or a routing loop, the whole system is lagging.
Connection to the database is failing randomly. Check the path and see if any switches are overloaded.
Are there any bandwidth hogs active right now? The internet speed is terrible.
Network performance dropped off a cliff just now. I need a quick diagnostic sweep.
Investigate why the connection between the web tier and app tier is so slow.
We have a weird issue where only some hosts are slow. Check the top users and global flow count.
Something is wrong with s2, it's dropping packets. Check its load and power.
Find the root cause of the latency spikes in the core network.
Compare the current CPU load and power consumption between core switch s1 and edge switch s5.
What is the CPU usage of s2, s3, and s4?
I need to see the flow entry counts for both s1 and s6 before we push the new rules.
Compare the power consumption of s8 against s9.
Check if s3 has higher CPU load or more flows than s4.
Get me the active flow count and total power consumption of the whole datacenter.
Is s1 working harder than s2? Check their CPU and flow entries.
What's the status of the edge switches? Check CPU for s7 and s8.
I need to clear out some old rules. What flow entries are currently on s3 before I delete them?
Before I reboot s2, what is its current power consumption and flow count?
I'm going to modify the flows on s5. Let me see the active flows and its CPU load first.
Get the path from h1 to h10 before I install the new reroute rules.
Before disabling s4, check how many flows it's handling right now.
I want to nuke the flows on s1, but first show me the top 3 bandwidth users to see if it's justified.
Show me the bandwidth utilization between s1 and s2 before I bring the link down.
I'm planning to power cycle s7. Pull its CPU stats and flow table.
Check the total network power consumption before I turn on the backup switches.
What are the top 5 congested links? I need to know before adding new routing policies.
Traffic from h2 to h8 is dropping. Map the path and check if there are any massive flows hogging the network right now.
Give me a quick morning health audit of the entire system: total power, total active flows, and global switch CPU utilization.
Check the overall network status. I need the top 3 users and the total power consumption.
Is the network under heavy load? Show me global CPU utilization and the 5 most congested links.
Provide a full summary: active flow count, top 10 flows, and total power.
Identify the bottleneck: get the path from h3 to h4 and the 3 largest flows in the system.
Audit the core layer: show CPU for s1, s2 and the total network active flows.
We are planning capacity. Give me the top 10 users and total power consumption.
Before I nuke the core switch, get the path from the web tier to the database, ping the database, fetch the top 10 flows, and grab the logs from s1.
Get real time traffic on edge switch s5 port 2 for 45 seconds, then backup the configurations for both aggregation switches.
The app tier is compromised. Force a mitigation: block the app server, install a meter entry on s4 to drop traffic, and clear s4 logs.
Conduct a deep audit. Get the MAC and ARP tables for edge switch s7, check port statistics for its uplink port, and list the top 15 bandwidth users globally.
Traffic is dropping between the app tier and database. Check the path switch count, get the bandwidth utilization between s2 and s3 on port 1 for 120s, and check flow entry counts on both.
We need to isolate the web tier. Reroute all traffic matching dst_ip 192.168.1.10 to a new path through s1 and s2, then delete the old flow entries on the core switch.
Push a new flow entry to s2, install a group entry for multicast on s2, and update the firmware on s2 without confirmation.
I need to monitor the live traffic on the core switch to see what's causing the spike. Check the top 5 congested links and ping the web server.
The network is melting down. Get the total power consumption, active flow count, top 10 flows, link latency from web to app, and packet loss rate from web to app.
Do a health check on the core and aggregation switches. Get device health for s1, s2, and s3. Also check the switch temperature and memory utilization for s1.
Before I flush the routing tables, backup the configuration for s1, get the current network topology, and fetch the logs from s1.
A DDoS attack is detected. Force a mitigation: block host h_attacker, toggle historical logging to 'on', set port status of s5 port 1 to 'down', and clear logs on s1.
Monitor real-time traffic on edge switch s6 port 3 for 60 seconds. At the same time, check its cpu utilization, power consumption, and get its flow entries.
Check the path from the web tier to the database. I am planning to reroute it later, but for now just show me the switch capabilities of the core switch and get all hosts.
